We all experience FOMO, the fear of missing out. This innate fear of losing access to desirable opportunities or resources is reflected in many human behaviors. Let's take just one example. A few years ago in New York City, cupcakes that ordinarily sold for $3.95 suddenly started selling for $250 apiece. Why would people pay that much for a cupcake and that much more than the day before? Because the famous bakery that produced the beloved treats announced that it was going out of business that day. That's why customers paid up to $250 online just to get one of the last of the cupcakes after the bakery closed its doors forever. Just as the principle of scarcity would predict.
